command_examples: ffuf --input-cmd 'radamsa --seed $FFUF_NUM example1.txt example2.txt' -H "Content-Type: application/json" -X POST -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL -mc all -fc 400

documentation.readme: - Installation - Example usage - Content discovery - Vhost discovery - Parameter fuzzing - POST data fuzzing - Using external mutator - Configuration files - Help - Interactive mode
usage_sections: Installation: - Download a prebuilt binary from releases page, unpack and run! or - If you are on Windows with Scoop, ffuf can be installed with: scoop install ffuf or - If you are on Windows with Winget, ffuf can be installed with: winget install ffuf.ffuf or - If you are on macOS with homebr

arguments.name: -ac
arguments.description: Automatically calibrate filtering options (default: false)

arguments.name: -acc
arguments.description: Custom auto-calibration string. Can be used multiple times. Implies -ac

arguments.name: -ach
arguments.description: Per host autocalibration (default: false)

arguments.name: -ack
arguments.description: Autocalibration keyword (default: FUZZ)

arguments.name: -acs
arguments.description: Custom auto-calibration strategies. Can be used multiple times. Implies -ac

arguments.name: -b
arguments.description: Cookie data `"NAME1=VALUE1; NAME2=VALUE2"` for copy as curl functionality.

arguments.name: -c
arguments.description: Colorize output. (default: false)

arguments.name: -cc
arguments.description: Client cert for authentication. Client key needs to be defined as well for this to work

arguments.name: -ck
arguments.description: Client key for authentication. Client certificate needs to be defined as well for this to work

arguments.name: -config
arguments.description: Load configuration from a file

arguments.name: -d
arguments.description: POST data

arguments.name: -debug-log
arguments.description: Write all of the internal logging to the specified file.

arguments.name: -e
arguments.description: Comma separated list of extensions. Extends FUZZ keyword.

arguments.name: -enc
arguments.description: Encoders for keywords, eg. 'FUZZ:urlencode b64encode'

arguments.name: -fc
arguments.description: Filter HTTP status codes from response. Comma separated list of codes and ranges

arguments.name: -fl
arguments.description: Filter by amount of lines in response. Comma separated list of line counts and ranges

arguments.name: -fmode
arguments.description: Filter set operator. Either of: and, or (default: or)

arguments.name: -fr
arguments.description: Filter regexp

arguments.name: -fs
arguments.description: Filter HTTP response size. Comma separated list of sizes and ranges

arguments.name: -ft
arguments.description: Filter by number of milliseconds to the first response byte, either greater or less than. EG: >100 or <100

arguments.name: -fw
arguments.description: Filter by amount of words in response. Comma separated list of word counts and ranges

arguments.name: -http2
arguments.description: Use HTTP2 protocol (default: false)

arguments.name: -ic
arguments.description: Ignore wordlist comments (default: false)

arguments.name: -ignore-body
arguments.description: Do not fetch the response content. (default: false)

arguments.name: -input-cmd
arguments.description: Command producing the input. --input-num is required when using this input method. Overrides -w.

arguments.name: -input-num
arguments.description: Number of inputs to test. Used in conjunction with --input-cmd. (default: 100)

arguments.name: -input-shell
arguments.description: Shell to be used for running command

arguments.name: -json
arguments.description: JSON output, printing newline-delimited JSON records (default: false)

arguments.name: -maxtime
arguments.description: Maximum running time in seconds for entire process. (default: 0)

arguments.name: -maxtime-job
arguments.description: Maximum running time in seconds per job. (default: 0)

arguments.name: -mc
arguments.description: Match HTTP status codes, or "all" for everything. (default: 200-299,301,302,307,401,403,405,500)

arguments.name: -ml
arguments.description: Match amount of lines in response

arguments.name: -mmode
arguments.description: Matcher set operator. Either of: and, or (default: or)

arguments.name: -mode
arguments.description: Multi-wordlist operation mode. Available modes: clusterbomb, pitchfork, sniper (default: clusterbomb)

arguments.name: -mr
arguments.description: Match regexp

arguments.name: -ms
arguments.description: Match HTTP response size

arguments.name: -mt
arguments.description: Match how many milliseconds to the first response byte, either greater or less than. EG: >100 or <100

arguments.name: -mw
arguments.description: Match amount of words in response

arguments.name: -noninteractive
arguments.description: Disable the interactive console functionality (default: false)

arguments.name: -o
arguments.description: Write output to file

arguments.name: -od
arguments.description: Directory path to store matched results to.

arguments.name: -of
arguments.description: Output file format. Available formats: json, ejson, html, md, csv, ecsv (or, 'all' for all formats) (default: json)

arguments.name: -or
arguments.description: Don't create the output file if we don't have results (default: false)

arguments.name: -p
arguments.description: Seconds of `delay` between requests, or a range of random delay. For example "0.1" or "0.1-2.0"

arguments.name: -r
arguments.description: Follow redirects (default: false)

arguments.name: -rate
arguments.description: Rate of requests per second (default: 0)

arguments.name: -raw
arguments.description: Do not encode URI (default: false)

arguments.name: -recursion
arguments.description: Scan recursively. Only FUZZ keyword is supported, and URL (-u) has to end in it. (default: false)

arguments.name: -recursion-depth
arguments.description: Maximum recursion depth. (default: 0)

arguments.name: -replay-proxy
arguments.description: Replay matched requests using this proxy.

arguments.name: -request
arguments.description: File containing the raw http request

arguments.name: -request-proto
arguments.description: Protocol to use along with raw request (default: https)

arguments.name: -s
arguments.description: Do not print additional information (silent mode) (default: false)

arguments.name: -sa
arguments.description: Stop on all error cases. Implies -sf and -se. (default: false)

arguments.name: -scraperfile
arguments.description: Custom scraper file path

arguments.name: -scrapers
arguments.description: Active scraper groups (default: all)

arguments.name: -se
arguments.description: Stop on spurious errors (default: false)

arguments.name: -search
arguments.description: Search for a FFUFHASH payload from ffuf history

arguments.name: -sf
arguments.description: Stop when > 95% of responses return 403 Forbidden (default: false)

arguments.name: -sni
arguments.description: Target TLS SNI, does not support FUZZ keyword

examples: ffuf --input-cmd 'radamsa --seed $FFUF_NUM example1.txt example2.txt' -H "Content-Type: application/json" -X POST -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL -mc all -fc 400

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zodiacon/HookHunter (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zmap/zmap (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the exploit category. Fits when the engagement needs: - Installation - Example usage - Content discovery - Vhost discovery - Parameter fuzzing - POST data fuzzing - Using external mutator - Configuration files - Help - Interactive mode.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: - Installation - Example usage - Content discovery - Vhost discovery - Parameter fuzzing - POST data fuzzing - Using external mutator - Configuration files - Help - Interactive mode.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `ffuf -w /path/to/wordlist -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. Further invocations from upstream: `ffuf -w /path/to/vhost/wordlist -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL -H "Host: FUZZ" -fs 4242`; `ffuf -w /path/to/paramnames.txt -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL -fs 4242`. - Installation - Example usage - Content discovery - Vhost discovery - Parameter fuzzing - POST data fuzzing - Using external mutator - Configuration files - Help - Interactive mode.

name: ffuf
summary: - Installation - Example usage - Content discovery - Vhost discovery - Parameter fuzzing - POST data fuzzing - Using external mutator - Configuration files - Help - Interactive mode

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need exploit, use ffuf because - Installation - Example usage - Content discovery - Vhost discovery - Parameter fuzzing - POST data fuzzing - Using external mutator - Configuration files - He.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ffuf -w /path/to/wordlist -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for ffuf.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ffuf -w /path/to/vhost/wordlist -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL -H "Host: FUZZ" -fs 4242` as documented upstream for ffuf.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ffuf -w /path/to/paramnames.txt -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL -fs 4242` as documented upstream for ffuf.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ffuf -w /path/to/values.txt -u $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL -fc 401` as documented upstream for ffuf.
When chaining exploit tools, pair ffuf with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ffuf --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
